title: 10 best herbal tinctures for cracked heels
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for cracked heels are concentrated extracts of healing herbs, typically created by soaking plant material in alcohol or glycerin. These tinctures help with cracked heels due to their moisturizing, anti-inflammatory, and skin-repairing properties. Examples of herbal tinctures that benefit cracked heels include calendula, comfrey, and St. John's wort. Calendula provides moisture and anti-inflammatory relief, while comfrey encourages skin cell regeneration. St. John's wort has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infection in cracked heels. By incorporating these herbal tinctures into foot care routines, individuals can experience relief from cracked heels, reducing pain, and improving mobility, ultimately enhancing their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common sunflower tincture helps with cracked heels because it contains a high concentration of nutrients and antioxidants that promote skin health. The tincture is extracted from the flowers, leaves, and stems of the sunflower plant, which are rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as essential fatty acids. These nutrients work together to moisturize and nourish dry, cracked skin, while also reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair. Additionally, the antioxidants found in sunflower tincture help protect the skin from environmental stressors that can contribute to cracked heels. By using sunflower tincture regularly, you can help soothe and heal cracked heels, leaving your feet feeling soft and smooth.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: This property is important for cracked heels as it helps to prevent infection in the cracked skin, and the active constituents responsible for this property include sunflower kinins and polyphenols.
Emollient property: This property helps to soothe and moisturize the skin, promoting healing and preventing further cracking, and is attributed to the presence of fatty acids in sunflower oil.

Anti-inflammatory property: This property is crucial for cracked heels as it reduces inflammation and irritation, providing relief and aiding in the healing process, and is due to the presence of vitamin E and other antioxidants in sunflower oil.

Wound-healing property: This property is essential for cracked heels as it promotes tissue regeneration and speeds up healing, and is attributed to the presence of sunflower kinins, vitamin E, and other nutrients.

Circulation-boosting property: This property is important for cracked heels as it improves blood flow to the feet, promoting healing and preventing dryness, and is due to the presence of essential fatty acids and vitamin E in sunflower oil.
remedy_recipe: Harvest common sunflower (Helianthus annuus) flower heads at peak bloom, ensuring they are fully open and free of pesticides or pollutants.
Chop or grind 2 cups of sunflower flowers, placing them in a clean, dry glass jar, then add 1 cup of 80-proof vodka and 1 cup of distilled water, sealing the jar tightly.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2-4 weeks in a cool, dark location, occasionally shaking the jar gently to ensure even extraction.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the marc (plant material) to extract all liquid; store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.
Apply the sunflower tincture to cracked heels daily, massaging it gently into the affected area until fully absorbed, allowing the herbal properties to promote healing and skin regeneration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with cracked heels because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe and heal damaged skin. The active compounds in pot marigold, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, promote tissue regeneration and improve skin elasticity, which can help to reduce the appearance of cracked heels. Additionally, the tincture's astringent properties can help to tighten and tone the skin, preventing further dryness and cracking. Its emollient properties also help to lock in moisture, keeping the skin soft and supple. Overall, pot marigold tincture is a natural and effective remedy for treating cracked heels and promoting healthy, smooth skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and redness in cracked heels, providing relief and promoting healing. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and triterpenoids.

Wound-healing: The wound-healing property of pot marigold tincture helps accelerate the healing process of cracked heels by stimulating tissue regeneration. This is attributed to the presence of the active constituent, lutein.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tincture's antimicrobial property helps prevent infection in cracked heels by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi. The active constituents responsible for this property are mainly volatile oils and flavonoids.

Emollient: The emollient property of pot marigold tincture helps soften and moisturize dry, cracked skin, promoting suppleness and elasticity. This property is due to the presence of mucilage and waxes in the plant.
Analgesic: Pot marigold tincture's analgesic property helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with cracked heels. This property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and triterpenoids.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 cups of dried marigold flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 quart of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the marigold flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid and discarding the marigold solids.
Store the final tincture in a clean glass bottle, label it with the ingredients and date, and use it as needed for cracked heels.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with cracked heels because it has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that promote healing and skin cell regeneration. The astringent properties of yarrow tincture help to tighten and dry out the skin, reducing excess moisture that can lead to cracked heels. Additionally, yarrow tincture contains antioxidants that help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall skin health. The anti-inflammatory properties of yarrow tincture also help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with cracked heels, providing relief and promoting faster healing. Yarrow tincture can be applied directly to the affected area or added to a foot bath to soothe and heal cracked heels.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Yarrow tincture's astringent property helps to tighten and dry out excessively cracked skin, reducing the chances of infection and promoting faster healing. This is due to the presence of tannins in yarrow.

Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with cracked heels, providing relief and allowing for faster recovery. This is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones in yarrow.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tincture's antimicrobial property helps prevent and treat potential infections in cracked heels by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi. This is due to the presence of volatile oils like azulene and camphor in yarrow.

Wound-healing: Yarrow tincture's wound-healing property promotes skin cell regeneration and tissue repair, which helps to mend cracked heels. This is attributed to the presence of alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins in yarrow.

Analgesic: Yarrow tincture's analgesic property helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with cracked heels, making it easier for individuals to carry out daily activities. This is due to the presence of lactones, alkaloids, and flavonoids in yarrow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, rinse, and let dry for 24 hours.
Chop the dried yarrow into small pieces and place in a clean jar, filling it up to three-quarters full.
Pour 10 ounces of 80-proof vodka or grain alcohol into the jar, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it gently once a day.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid, and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tincture helps with cracked heels because it contains allantoin, a substance that promotes the growth of new skin cells and helps to heal wounds. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain associated with cracked heels. The tincture can be applied directly to the affected area, providing instant relief and hydration to the dry, cracked skin. Regular use of comfrey tincture can help to soften and smooth the skin on the feet, preventing further cracking and promoting overall foot health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to allantoin and rosmarinic acid, help reduce swelling and redness in cracked heels.
Wound healing: Comfrey tincture promotes wound healing, thanks to allantoin, which accelerates skin cell regeneration and tissue repair.
Pain relief: The analgesic effect of comfrey tincture, attributed to phenolic acids, alleviates discomfort and pain caused by cracked heels.
Moisturizing: Comfrey tincture's moisturizing properties, resulting from mucilage content, help soothe and hydrate dry, cracked skin.
Antimicrobial: Comfrey tincture's antimicrobial properties, provided by tannins and rosmarinic acid, protect cracked heels from potential infections.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried comfrey root into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Pour 1 pint of 100-proof vodka over the comfrey root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently once a day.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the resulting tincture in a glass dropper bottle and apply a few drops to cracked heels daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica flower tincture helps with cracked heels because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can soothe and heal damaged skin. Arnica stimulates the circulation of blood and oxygen to the affected area, promoting the healing process and reducing inflammation. Its regenerative properties help to repair and rejuvenate dry, cracked skin, leaving it soft, smooth, and moisturized. Furthermore, arnica tincture has been shown to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with cracked heels, making it an effective natural remedy for this common foot problem. Regular use of arnica tincture can help prevent the recurrence of cracked heels and keep feet healthy and looking their best.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica flower tincture reduces inflammation, which helps alleviate the pain and discomfort caused by cracked heels, thanks to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids.

Improves blood circulation: Arnica stimulates blood flow, aiding in the healing process of cracked heels by promoting nutrient and oxygen supply to the affected area, due to the presence of volatile oils.

Pain relief: Arnica has analgesic properties that help alleviate the pain associated with cracked heels, as it contains helenalin and dihydrohelenalin which inhibit the production of pain-inducing substances.
Antimicrobial: Arnica flower tincture inhibits bacterial growth, reducing the risk of infection in cracked heels, because of its phenolic acids and tannins.
Tissue repair: Arnica promotes tissue regeneration, helping to repair and rejuvenate damaged skin in cracked heels, due to its essential oils, carotenoids, and triterpenes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh Arnica montana flowers, avoiding the stem and leaves, then rinse and let dry for 12 hours.
Fill a clean jar with the dried flowers, adding 80-proof vodka until the flowers are completely submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid, and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.
Apply the tincture to cracked heels with a cotton ball or spray bottle, allowing it to air dry before covering with socks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog rose tincture helps with cracked heels because it contains high levels of vitamin C and antioxidants that promote skin health and regeneration. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe and reduce irritation, while its astringent properties can help tighten and strengthen the skin, preventing further cracking. Additionally, the tincture's emollient properties can moisturize and soften the skin, providing relief from dryness and roughness. Regular use of herbal dog rose tincture can help maintain healthy and supple skin on the heels, preventing and healing cracked heels.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dog rose tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of tannins and flavonoids, can help reduce swelling and redness in cracked heels, promoting healing.
Astringent: The astringent properties of dog rose tincture, caused by tannins, can help tighten and dry out excessively moist skin, reducing the likelihood of cracked heels.
Antimicrobial: Dog rose tincture's antimicrobial properties, resulting from its vitamin C and bioflavonoid content, can help prevent infections in cracked heels.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of dog rose tincture, due to the presence of tannins and essential fatty acids, can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with cracked heels.
Antioxidant: Dog rose tincture's antioxidant properties, resulting from its high vitamin C content, can help promote skin cell regeneration, aiding in the healing of cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 ounces of fresh dog rose (Rosa canina) petals and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of 80-proof vodka or rum over the petals, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid and discarding the petals.
Store the finished tincture in a sterilized glass bottle, keeping it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Apply the tincture to cracked heels as needed.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of hypericin and hyperforin, can help reduce swelling and redness in cracked heels, providing relief and promoting healing.
Wound-healing: The wound-healing properties of St. John's wort, attributed to its tannins and flavonoids, can help speed up the healing process of cracked heels by promoting tissue regeneration.

Pain-relieving: The pain-relieving properties of St. John's wort, due to its active constituents like hypericin and hyperforin, can help alleviate discomfort caused by cracked heels, making it easier to manage the condition.

Antimicrobial: St. John's wort tincture's antimicrobial properties, due to its hypericin and hyperforin content, can help prevent infection in cracked heels, ensuring a safe and healthy healing process.

Moisturizing: The moisturizing properties of St. John's wort, due to its flavonoids and essential oils, can help keep the skin hydrated and supple, preventing further cracking and promoting overall skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, filling a clean, dry jar about 3/4 full.
Pour 100 proof vodka over the herbs until they are completely submerged, covering the jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, discarding the plant material and transferring the liquid to a clean, dark glass bottle.
Label the bottle with the name of the tincture, the date, and dosage instructions (e.g., apply a few drops to cracked heels as needed).
remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with cracked heels because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe and heal dry, cracked skin. The herb's active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, have been shown to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for treating cracked heels. Additionally, St. John's wort tincture can improve blood flow to the feet, providing essential nutrients and oxygen to the affected area, which can further aid in the healing process. Its moisturizing properties can also help to soften and hydrate the skin, preventing further cracking and providing long-lasting relief.

Plantain tincture helps with cracked heels because it has anti-inflammatory, astringent, and wound-healing properties. The astringent properties of plantain help to tighten and strengthen the skin, reducing the likelihood of cracking. Its anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe any irritation or discomfort associated with cracked heels. Furthermore, plantain tincture has been found to promote wound healing, which can help to speed up the recovery process of cracked heels. The herb's mucilage content creates a protective barrier that locks in moisture, keeping the skin hydrated and supple, and preventing further cracking. Overall, plantain tincture is a natural and effective remedy for treating and preventing cracked heels.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Plantain tincture's antimicrobial properties help prevent cracked heels from becoming infected, as the active constituents, such as aucubin and allamandin, can inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi.
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory effects, due to compounds like flavonoids and tannins, help reduce inflammation and irritation, providing relief for cracked and sore heels.
Wound healing: Plantain tincture promotes wound healing, thanks to its allantoin content, which encourages skin cell regeneration and tissue repair.
Emollient: Plantain has emollient properties, meaning it can soften and soothe dry, cracked skin on heels by creating a protective barrier that locks in moisture.
Astringent: The astringent properties of plantain tincture, resulting from tannins, help tighten skin cells and reduce excess skin peeling, providing relief and protection for cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest plantain leaves from a healthy, pesticide-free source, rinse, and dry thoroughly.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of dried plantain leaves and place them in a clean, dry jar.
Pour 16 ounces of 100-proof vodka or pure grain alcohol over the plantain leaves, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid, and store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet violet tincture helps with cracked heels because it contains anti-inflammatory and demulcent properties that can soothe and heal dry, cracked skin. The herb's mucilage content creates a protective barrier on the skin, locking in moisture and promoting hydration. Additionally, sweet violet has antimicrobial and antifungal properties that can help prevent infection in cracked heels. Its soothing and cooling effects can alleviate discomfort and irritation, making it an ideal natural remedy for treating and preventing cracked heels. Regular application of sweet violet tincture can help keep the skin soft, supple, and healthy.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sweet violet tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of salicylic acid, helps reduce inflammation and irritation in cracked heels.
Emollient: Its emollient property, from the mucilage content, helps to soothe and soften dry, cracked skin, promoting healing.
Analgesic: The analgesic effect of sweet violet tincture, attributed to salicylic acid, helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with cracked heels.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property, owing to the saponins content, helps protect cracked heels from potential infections, ensuring proper healing.

Circulation-enhancing: Sweet violet tincture's circulation-enhancing property, due to the flavonoids content, promotes blood flow, aiding in the healing and regeneration of skin tissue in cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of fresh sweet violet flowers and gently rinse them to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the flowers and place them in a clean glass jar, then pour 2 cups of vodka or rum over the flowers until they are completely submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the specified time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer into a clean glass bottle.
Label the bottle with the name, date, and dosage instructions, and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Apply the tincture to cracked heels as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tincture helps with cracked heels because it contains nutrients and antioxidants that promote skin health. The herb is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like potassium and zinc, which are essential for skin repair and regeneration. Dandelion tincture also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with dry, cracked heels. Furthermore, the tincture's potent antibacterial and antifungal agents can help prevent infections that can exacerbate the condition. Regular use of dandelion tincture can help soothe, moisturize, and protect the skin, promoting the healing of cracked heels and improving their overall appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with cracked heels.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of dandelion tincture, attributed to its phenolic compounds, can help prevent infections in cracked heels by inhibiting the growth of microorganisms.
Moisturizing: Dandelion tincture's moisturizing properties, resulting from its richness in essential fatty acids, can help soothe and hydrate dry, cracked skin on the heels.

Wound-healing: The wound-healing properties of dandelion tincture, owing to its content of taraxasterol and other triterpenes, can help promote tissue regeneration and speed up the healing process in cracked heels.

Antioxidant: Dandelion tincture's antioxidant properties, attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect skin cells from oxidative stress, supporting overall skin health and resilience in the face of cracked heels.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh dandelion plants, including flowers, leaves, and roots, ensuring they are grown in a pesticide-free environment.
Thoroughly clean and chop the dandelion plants, then fill a clean mason jar ¾ full with the chopped herbs.
Pour 80-proof vodka or rum into the jar, completely covering the dandelion herbs and leaving a 1-inch space at the top.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

After the designated time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid. Store the resulting dandelion tincture in a dark glass bottle and apply it topically to cracked heels as needed.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat cracked heels with herbal tinctures is to use calendula or plantain tinctures. These herbs possess anti-inflammatory and healing properties that can soothe and repair damaged skin. Dilute the tincture in warm water and soak your feet for 15-20 minutes daily. After soaking, gently exfoliate the cracked skin using a pumice stone or foot file, then moisturize thoroughly. Regularly applying a tincture-based salve or balm can also promote healing and prevent further cracking.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cracked heels that are treated with herbal tinctures include dry, rough skin and fungal infections. For dry, rough skin, tinctures made from calendula, comfrey, and plantain can help moisturize and soothe the affected area. Meanwhile, for fungal infections like athlete's foot, tinctures containing ingredients like tea tree oil, goldenseal, and myrrh can help alleviate symptoms and promote healing. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before using any herbal remedies to ensure they are safe and effective for your specific condition.
